question.text: Under SEBI regulations, what is the maximum percentage of excess equity shares that can be allotted under the Green Shoe Option (GSO)?

explanation.text: **The Logic:** The Green Shoe Option allows allotment of equity shares in EXCESS of the issue size, up to a maximum of 15%. This is a post-listing price stabilization mechanism where shares are returned to promoters/pre-issue shareholders within 2 working days after the stabilization period ends. **The Trap:** 10% is a common distractor because candidates confuse GSO limits with other SEBI thresholds. 20% is tempting for those who round up the figure. **Cross-Exam Context:** GSO concepts are tested in NISM Series IX specifically. NISM Series VIII may reference stabilization in derivatives context. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** Think of GSO as an "insurance policy" for the issuer — it helps stabilize post-listing prices by allowing up to 15% overallotment.
